studiecafe.title: Study Café
studiecafe.subtitle: Ask a question, share a tip, or start a discussion
studiecafe.notifications.title: Email notifications
studiecafe.notifications.subtitle: Choose what you want to be emailed about.
studiecafe.notifications.repliesHint: An email when someone replies to a conversation you started.
studiecafe.notifications.announcementsHi: An email when a teacher posts an announcement.
studiecafe.notifications.digestNote: Notifications are bundled together so a busy conversation won't flood your inbox.
studiecafe.noCourse: Pick a course first to open the study café.
studiecafe.empty: No conversations yet. Start the first one!
studiecafe.lockedHint: This conversation is closed to new replies.
studiecafe.noReplies: No replies yet. Be the first!
studiecafe.replyAttachment.bannerTitle: Post this AI answer as a reply
studiecafe.replyAttachment.bannerHint: Open an existing topic below to post this answer as a reply.
studiecafe.replyAttachment.includedHint: This AI answer will be included as an attachment.
studiecafe.loadError: The study café could not be loaded.
studiecafe.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this?
studiecafe.compose.bodyPlaceholder: What would you like to talk about?
studiecafe.formula.title: Formula
studiecafe.formula.hint: Write math with LaTeX between $…$ (inline) or $$…$$ (block).
studiecafe.sort.label: Sort
studiecafe.unread.navTitle: {count} new posts in the study café
studiecafe.unread.bannerOne: 1 conversation has new activity since your last visit.
studiecafe.unread.bannerMany: {count} conversations have new activity since your last visit.
studiecafe.unread.bannerAnnouncement: There is a new announcement from your teacher.
login.switchToSignIn: Already have an account? Sign in
login.firstUserNote: The first user is automatically granted admin access. All other registrations are added as students.
login.err.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 6 characters
login.err.invalidCredentials: Invalid login credentials. Check your email and password.
login.err.alreadyRegistered: This email address is already registered. Try signing in.
login.err.emailNotConfirmed: Email not yet confirmed. Check your inbox.
login.signUpSuccess: Account created! Check your email (including your spam folder) to confirm your account, then sign in.
login.forgotSubtitle: Enter your email address to reset your password
login.forgotSent: If this address is known to us, a reset email has been sent. Check your inbox (and your spam folder).
reset.title: Set a new password
reset.subtitle: Choose a new password for your account
reset.success: Your password has been changed. Redirecting…
reset.noSession: This reset link is invalid or has expired. Request a new reset email via "Forgot your password?".
activate.title: Activate account
activate.subtitle: Choose a password to activate your account
activate.passwordHint: At least 8 characters, including at least one letter and one digit.
activate.success: Your account has been activated. Redirecting…
activate.noSession: This activation link is invalid or has expired. Ask your teacher for a new invitation.
activate.err.tooWeak: Password too weak: use at least 8 characters, including at least one letter and one digit.
addUsers.title: Add users
addUsers.subtitle: Bulk-create student accounts from an email list and enroll them in one course.
addUsers.emailPrereq: Note: students receive an activation email. For large groups, custom SMTP must be configured in Supabase, and the /activate page must be in the list of allowed redirect URLs. Without these settings, emails may be blocked or rate-limited.
addUsers.emailsPlaceholder: Paste email addresses here, separated by comma, semicolon, space or newline…
addUsers.noAccess: You do not have access to this feature.
addUsers.err.noEmails: Enter at least one valid email address.

chat.title: Chat
chat.noProfile: Failed to load profile. Refresh the page.

chat.startNewToBegin: Start a new chat to begin
chat.askQuestion: Ask a question about epidemiology or biostatistics
chat.profileLoadFailedDetail: Something went wrong while loading your profile. This may be due to a connection problem or a technical issue.
chat.deleteClosingText: You are about to permanently delete the conversation "{title}". This cannot be undone.
chat.deleteAskSummary: Would you like the learning assistant to first save a formative summary of this conversation to your learning journal? You can review it later to reflect.
explain.title: I Explain
explain.subtitle: Choose a concept and explain it in your own words. You will receive detailed feedback!
explain.selectConceptSub: Choose a concept from the list to get started
explain.explainInOwnWords: Explain the concept in your own words
explain.explainLabel: Explain this concept in your own words
explain.noConceptsForCourse: No concepts yet for this course
explain.noConceptsForCourseHint: Ask the administrator to extract concepts via the Admin panel (RAG settings).
explain.globalConceptsBanner: You are viewing the general concept list — these are not specific to a course. Select a course to see course-specific concepts.
explain.historyHint: Click an explanation to review it. Delete or move it to your journal.
explain.noHistoryYet: No explanations yet. Start with a concept from the list.
explain.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this explanation?
explain.externalKnowledgeDesc: Part of this feedback (marked with "(beyond course material)") is based on the language model's general knowledge and not on your course files. For those sentences in particular, it is wise to check with your lecturer before including them in an exam or assignment.
explain.sources.evidenceBadgeTitle: This fragment was captured as a course-material source during concept extraction.
quiz.title: Quiz
quiz.subtitle: Choose one or more topics and a question type. The learning assistant generates a quiz, evaluates your open answers with feedback + feed forward, and places completed quizzes at the bottom of this page.
quiz.question: Question
quiz.noTopicsAvailable: No topics are available for this course yet. Ask your lecturer to add concepts first.
quiz.wellDone: Well done! You have successfully completed the quiz.
quiz.keepPractising: Keep practising! You need at least 70% to pass.
quiz.noQuizzesYet: You haven't completed any quizzes yet. Start your first quiz above.
quiz.savedToJournal: Note saved to your learning journal
quiz.noConceptsGlobalNote: No specific topics for the active course yet — showing the general list.
projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Work in groups on a research project. You can start fresh or continue your previous work.
projects.noProjectsDesc: There are no projects available for your course yet.
projects.restartConfirm: Are you sure you want to start a new project? Your current session will be preserved.
projects.notLinked: You are not yet linked to a course with projects. Ask your lecturer to add you.
projects.noActiveProjects: No active projects in this course yet.
projects.enterInviteCode: Enter the invite code you received from a group member.
feedback.title: Learning Journal
feedback.emptyChat: No chat notes yet. Close a conversation and choose "Move to learning journal" for an automatic summary — or add your own note here any time.
feedback.emptyExplain: No notes from I Explain yet. Explain a concept in your own words and archive the feedback — or add your own note here any time.
feedback.emptyQuiz: No quiz notes yet. Complete a quiz and choose "Save summary" — or add your own note here any time.
feedback.emptyProject: No project notes yet. Run a checkpoint in the project room — or add your own note here any time.
feedback.emptyOther: No other notes yet. Add your own note here any time.
feedback.deleteConfirm: Do you want to delete this note?
resources.title: Resources
resources.courseFilesDesc: Documents that serve as a knowledge source for Chat, Explain and Quiz.
resources.otherFilesDesc: Datasets and other files made available by the lecturer.
room.title: Project Room
room.noMessages: No messages yet — start the conversation with your group.
room.review.title: Verdicts
room.relationship.blockedBanner: This persona wants things repaired first — ask your teacher to correct the relationship before you can continue.
room.relationship.panelHint: Score ranges from -10 (cold) to +10 (warm). At ≤ -8 the persona blocks new conversations.
room.relationship.adjustSub: Add a correction to the score (delta between -10 and +10) and briefly explain why.
room.relationship.notePlaceholder: For example: conversation normalised after reconsideration.
room.consultations.blockedBanner: Your group has reached the maximum of {limit} consultations with this persona. Ask your teacher for extra consultations if you want to continue.
room.consultations.confirmBody: This starts a new conversation and counts as one consultation. You will have {remaining} of {limit} left afterwards.
room.consultations.confirmBodyLast: Note: this is your last consultation with this persona ({limit} in total). You will not be able to start a new conversation after this.
room.consultations.grantSub: Give this group extra consultations on top of the persona limit. This is the total number of extras (0–1000).
room.consultations.grantNotePlaceholder: Why is this group getting extra consultations?
room.consultations.autoCloseNote: Idle conversations close automatically after {hours} h.
room.closeConversationSub: Below you can see the summary the AI has made of this conversation. Review it and then close.
room.noAgreements: No explicit agreements identified in this conversation.
room.finalReflectionSub: Write a joint final reflection. The supervisor gives feedback per rubric criterion and adds it to everyone's learning journal.
room.reflectionPlaceholder: What have you done? What do you still not understand? What is the next step?
room.checkpointSub: Below are automatic summaries of your conversations. Adjust them if you wish and save them to your learning journals.
room.assessmentTitle: Ask the assessor for formative feedback on your conversations
room.emojiTip: Tip: use your OS emoji picker (Win+. or Cmd+Ctrl+Space).
quiz.answerPlaceholder: Write your answer in full sentences here…
quiz.openQuestionsNote: For open questions and cases the number is limited — each answer is evaluated by the language model.
quiz.casusItemBankNote: ItemBank contains no cases — for this question type this source is skipped and supplemented with creative LLM questions.
quiz.ragFolderNote: If a concept shows "RAG: —", no primary course folder is linked yet and the generator falls back to general course folders.
room.checkpointSavedSub: The summaries are in your learning journals. The project is still open — you can continue with the conversations.
quiz.type.mcq.label: Multiple choice
quiz.type.mcq.subtitle: 4 options, direct feedback
quiz.type.open.label: Open question
quiz.type.open.subtitle: Free text, AI evaluation with percentage score
quiz.type.casus.label: Case
quiz.type.casus.subtitle: Problem sketch + open answer, AI evaluation
quiz.migrationError: The quiz database has not yet been updated to the new model. Your administrator must apply migration 20260430120000_extend_quiz_attempts_for_multi_type.sql in Supabase. Until then you can still generate and practise quizzes, but completed attempts will not be saved.
quiz.noQuestionsFromSources: No questions could be compiled from the selected sources.
quiz.errorGenerating: An error occurred while generating the quiz.
quiz.historyNotSavedMigration: Your quiz could not be saved to your history because the quiz database has not yet been updated to the new model.
quiz.historyNotSaved: Your quiz could not be saved to your history.
quiz.applyMigrationHint: Ask your administrator to apply migration 20260430120000_extend_quiz_attempts_for_multi_type.sql in Supabase.
quiz.deletedSummaryFailed: The quiz was deleted, but the summary could not be saved to your learning journal. Please try again later.
quiz.itemBankAtLeast: At least {count} ItemBank question(s){split} — exact count capped
quiz.llmAlwaysAvailable: LLM-creative is always available — Groq generates application and transfer questions without an external source
quiz.lastMixCounts: Previous quiz contained: {rag} course · {itembank} ItemBank · {llm} creative
quiz.contextStatsSome: {used} of {total} found passages were sent (the rest was skipped to keep the prompt within the limit).
quiz.contextStatsTrimmed: All {total} found passages were sent, but one passage was trimmed to keep the prompt within the limit.
quiz.contextStatsAll: All {total} found passages were available for the language model.
quiz.howItWorksMcqAfter: you see correct/incorrect directly per question.
quiz.howItWorksOpenAfter: you type an answer; you receive feedback, feed forward and a score of 0–100.
quiz.howItWorksCasusAfter: you first read a short problem sketch, then the same as open questions applies.
quiz.howItWorksHistory: Completed quizzes appear at the bottom of this page. You can always delete them, optionally with a note in your learning journal.
quiz.contextStatsNoteSome: Note: {used} of {total} found passages were sent to the language model (highest-scoring first).
quiz.contextStatsNoteTrimmed: Note: all {total} found passages were sent, but the content of one passage was trimmed to keep the prompt within the limit.
quiz.basedOnSourcesSingular: Based on {count} source from course material
quiz.basedOnSourcesPlural: Based on {count} sources from course material
quiz.quizCompletedSubtitle: You have completed the quiz about "{topics}"
quiz.readJournalBefore: You can read it back later on the
quiz.deleteQuizTitle: Delete and optionally save note to learning journal
quiz.noDetailData: No detail data available for this quiz.
quiz.deleteConfirmText: You are about to permanently delete the quiz "{label}". This cannot be undone.
quiz.deleteQuestion: Would you like the learning assistant to first save a formative summary of this quiz to your learning journal? That summary includes your results, feedback and feed forward, which you can revisit later for reflection.
explain.deletedSummaryFailed: The explanation was removed, but the summary could not be saved to your journal. Please try again later.
explain.submitError: An error occurred while submitting your explanation.
explain.notSavedAfter: saved. You can adjust it and try again.
explain.deleteConfirmBefore: You are about to permanently delete your explanation of
explain.deleteQuestion: Would you like the learning assistant to save a formative summary (with strengths, areas for improvement, and a follow-up suggestion) to your journal? You can review it later to reflect on your learning.
projects.restartConfirmAfter: will be left and you will exit the group.
projects.restartBullet1: Your checkpoints and learning journal notes are kept.
projects.restartBullet2: The conversations in the project room will no longer be visible to you — your group members can still access them.
projects.restartBullet3: A new, empty project room with a new group will then start.
projects.restartQuestionBefore: Do you really want to start over instead of
room.addPersonaFirst: First add a real persona in the management panel.
room.reflectionTooShort: Write a reflection of at least 20 characters.
room.assessmentConfirm: Are you requesting a formative assessment? The assessor will read your conversations and add feedback to each learning journal.
room.assessmentComplete: Assessment complete — {count} feedback report(s) are in your learning journals.
room.assessmentNoFeedback: Assessment completed but no feedback was returned.
room.serverError: Server error ({status}): unexpected response. Please try again.
room.conversationClosed: Conversation closed and saved to the logbook.
room.cue.staffToast: Conversation closed. {name} → relationship {delta} — reason: "{reason}" (visible to teachers only).
room.cue.staffToastNeutral: Conversation closed. {name} → relationship unchanged (visible to teachers only).
room.noClosedConversationsBefore: No closed conversations yet. Use the
room.noClosedConversationsAfter: button in the chat to close a conversation.
admin.title: Admin
admin.learningLevels.title: Student learning levels
admin.learningLevels.intro: Overview of the self-chosen learning level (1–5) per student for {course}. Students set this themselves; you cannot change it.
admin.learningLevels.readonlyNote: Read-only: the student stays in control of their own level.
admin.learningLevels.noCourse: Pick an active course first to view learning levels.
admin.learningLevels.empty: No student has set a learning level for this course yet.
admin.learningLevels.emptyHint: Students without a set level use the default level {level}.
admin.learningLevels.warning: The learning-level table is missing in the database — run the migration.
courseInfo.title: Course info
courseInfo.intro: Configure the information block shown at the top of the Dashboard for {course}.
courseInfo.noCourse: Choose an active course first to manage course info.
courseInfo.placeholder: Write the information for students here. Use the buttons above for formatting.
courseInfo.previewEmpty: No text yet — the preview appears here.
courseInfo.toolbar.heading: Heading
courseInfo.linkUrlPrompt: Enter the URL for the link:
courseInfo.linkDialog.title: Add link
admin.imports.title: Imports
admin.imports.intro: Link external libraries to LEAP-VU here. A linked library becomes available across the whole app, but is not used automatically. Whether a course actually uses a library — and with which concept mappings — is configured per course under Quiz Sources.
admin.imports.moreComing: You can link other libraries here later.
admin.imports.usageNote: ShareStats is available across the app. Enable it per course under Quiz Sources to actually use its questions.
admin.imports.sources.sharestats.label: ShareStats
admin.imports.sources.sharestats.desc: Multiple-choice and open questions from the ShareStats item bank.
admin.imports.sources.website.label: Website
admin.imports.sources.website.desc: Pages from a learning environment as searchable RAG sources.
admin.imports.web.title: Import website
admin.imports.web.intro: Paste the address of a learning environment or documentation site. LEAP-VU discovers the underlying pages, extracts the readable text and stores it as searchable RAG sources in your active course. You choose which pages to include. Only import sites whose material you are allowed to use.
admin.imports.web.targetCourse: Target course: the imported pages go into the RAG folder of course {course}.
admin.imports.web.noCourse: No active course: switch to a course before importing so the sources are stored in the right place.
admin.imports.web.noticeDiscovering: Discovering pages of the web environment — this may take a moment.
admin.imports.web.noticeNoPages: No pages found for this URL. Check that the address is correct.
admin.imports.web.noticeNoCourse: Choose an active course first to store the sources in.
admin.imports.web.noticeSelectPage: Select at least one page to import.
admin.imports.web.noticeImportStarted: Import started for {count} page(s) — this may take a few minutes.
admin.noAccess.title: No Access
admin.noAccess.desc: You do not have access to the admin panel. Please contact the administrator.
admin.header.title: Admin Dashboard
admin.header.subtitleAdmin: Manage users, documents and system settings
admin.backfill.titleSingular: Automatic folder mapping could not link {failed} row
admin.backfill.titlePlural: Automatic folder mapping could not link {failed} rows
admin.backfill.fixText: to fix the folder mapping manually.

admin.users.confirmLastTeacherForce: Are you sure you want to leave this course without a lecturer? (Only admins can force this.)
admin.users.expandTeacherCourses: Show courses where this user is a lecturer
admin.users.noTeacherCourses: This user is not a lecturer in any course.
admin.users.deleteWarning: You are about to permanently delete the account of {name} ({email}). The account and all related data will be removed.
admin.concepts.subtitle: Manage concepts for the "I Explain" module
admin.concepts.tipText: Use the "RAG Management" tab to automatically extract concepts from course material.
admin.concepts.bulkDeleteError: {failed} of {total} concepts could not be deleted.
admin.concepts.noCourseConceptsEmpty: No course concepts — use RAG Management to extract them.
admin.prompts.explainDesc: evaluation tone and structure for student explanations
admin.prompts.subtitle: Manage system prompts per section of the learning environment.
admin.prompts.migrationDesc: To enable section management, run this SQL once in the Supabase dashboard (SQL Editor):
admin.prompts.migrationAfter: After running the SQL: restart the server. The explanation prompt will be created automatically and section management will be activated.
admin.prompts.chatSubtitle: one system prompt for the chatbot
admin.prompts.explainSubtitle: evaluation tone and structure for student explanations
admin.prompts.noChatPrompt: No chat prompt found. Restart the server to create the default prompt.
admin.prompts.noExplainPrompt: No explanation prompt found. Run the migration SQL and restart the server to create the default prompt.
admin.prompts.explainPerCourse.title: Customise per course
admin.prompts.explainPerCourse.desc: Set a dedicated explanation prompt for a specific course. Without one, the course uses the global prompt above.
admin.prompts.explainPerCourse.placehold: Type the explanation prompt for this course…
admin.prompts.explainPerCourse.loadError: Failed to load the course prompt.
admin.prompts.chatPerCourse.title: Customise per course
admin.prompts.chatPerCourse.desc: Set a dedicated tutor-chat prompt for a specific course. Without one, the course uses the global prompt above.
admin.prompts.chatPerCourse.placeholder: Type the tutor-chat prompt for this course…
admin.prompts.chatPerCourse.loadError: Failed to load the course prompt.
admin.prompts.projectsSubtitle: one prompt per agent, freely customisable
admin.prompts.migrationRequired: Creating agent prompts is available after the database migration above has been run.
admin.prompts.agentContentPlaceholder: Describe the role and instructions of this agent...
admin.prompts.noAgentPrompts: No agent prompts created yet. Click "New agent prompt" to get started.
admin.ragSettings.title: RAG Proximity Settings
admin.ragSettings.subtitle: Configure per module how strictly RAG search results are filtered. Select a course or the global default below.
admin.ragSettings.courseOverrideNote: Course-specific settings override the global default.
admin.ragSettings.usesGlobal: uses the global default. Save to create a course-specific setting.
admin.ragSettings.globalNote: Global default — applies to all courses without their own setting.
admin.ragSettings.extractionDesc: When the AI regenerates the concept list, each candidate concept is verified against the course material. Concepts without sufficient evidence are rejected. Stricter = fewer but more relevant concepts.
admin.ragSettings.thresholdTip: Minimum similarity per candidate concept. Tip: with text-embedding-3-small, good matches typically score between 0.45 and 0.65.
admin.ragSettings.minEvidenceDesc: How many chunks must score above the threshold to accept the concept? Higher = stricter.
admin.ragSettings.strictSourceDesc: LLM may only answer based on the retrieved course texts
admin.ragSettings.enrichQueryDesc: Expands short concept names with synonyms, key_points and the definition before the embedding model is called. Prevents text-embedding-3-small from returning low similarity scores for isolated terms (e.g. "cohort"). Only active for "Explain concepts"; chat/quiz/project use their own context-rich queries and do not need this enrichment.
admin.ragSettings.diagDesc: Enter a search term (for example a concept name) to see which chunks are found — without applying the threshold. This lets you estimate what threshold is realistic for your course material.
admin.ragSettings.diagCourseRequired: Select a course above first — diagnosis requires a course selection for teachers.
admin.ragSettings.diagEnrich: Enrich the search term with synonyms (same logic as "Explain concepts" uses)
admin.ragSettings.diagDefinitionLabel: Optional: definition of the concept (as stored in concepts) for a fair comparison with "Explain concepts".
admin.ragSettings.diagDefinitionPlacehol: E.g. 'A group of people sharing a common characteristic who are followed over time.'
admin.ragSettings.diagNoChunks: No chunks found. Check whether the course has RAG folders assigned.
admin.ragSettings.overrideRemoved: Course override removed. Global default is now used.
admin.ragSettings.permissiveWarning: Very permissive — may retrieve irrelevant passages.
admin.settings.subtitle: System settings and configuration
admin.settings.apiDesc:
Configuration runs server-side via the VU Azure OpenAI resource:
- AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT + AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY (chat and embeddings)
- AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT for chat (e.g. gpt-5.5)
- AZURE_OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DEPLOYMENT for embeddings/RAG (text-embedding-3-small)
- GITHUB_TOKEN for higher GitHub API limits
admin.settings.apiConfigDesc:
Configuration runs server-side via the VU Azure OpenAI resource:
- AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT + AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY (chat and embeddings)
- AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT for chat (e.g. gpt-5.5)
- AZURE_OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DEPLOYMENT for embeddings/RAG (text-embedding-3-small)
- GITHUB_TOKEN for higher GitHub API limits
admin.prompts.chatDesc: one system prompt for the chatbot
admin.prompts.projectsDesc: one prompt per agent, freely customisable
admin.prompts.agentPromptsAfterMigration: Creating agent prompts is available after the database migration above has been run.
admin.concepts.tip: Use the "RAG Management" tab to automatically extract concepts from course material.
admin.concepts.noCourseConcepts: No course concepts — use RAG Management to extract them.
admin.ragSettings.overrideNote: Course-specific settings override the global default.
admin.ragSettings.globalDefaultDesc: Global default — applies to all courses without their own setting.
admin.ragSettings.extraction.title: Concept extraction
admin.ragSettings.extraction.desc: When the AI regenerates the concept list, each candidate concept is verified against the course material. Concepts without sufficient evidence are rejected. Stricter = fewer but more relevant concepts.
admin.ragSettings.extraction.thresholdDe: Minimum similarity per candidate concept. Tip: with text-embedding-3-small, good matches typically score between 0.45 and 0.65.
admin.ragSettings.extraction.minEvidence: How many chunks must score above the threshold to accept the concept? Higher = stricter.
admin.ragSettings.modules.permissiveWarn: Very permissive — may retrieve irrelevant passages.
admin.ragSettings.modules.strictDesc: LLM may only answer based on the retrieved course texts
admin.ragSettings.modules.expansionDesc: Expands short concept names with synonyms, key_points and the definition before the embedding model is called.
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.title: Diagnosis: test threshold
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.desc: Enter a search term (for example a concept name) to see which chunks are found — without applying the threshold. This lets you estimate what threshold is realistic for your course material.
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.selectCours: Select a course above first — diagnosis requires a course selection for teachers.
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.enrichLabel: Enrich the search term with synonyms (same logic as "Explain concepts" uses)
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.definitionL: Optional: definition of the concept (as stored in concepts) for a fair comparison with "Explain concepts".
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.definitionP: E.g. 'A group of people sharing a common characteristic who are followed over time.'
admin.ragSettings.diagnostic.noChunks: No chunks found. Check whether the course has RAG folders assigned.
admin.quizSources.selectCourseFirst: Select an active course first to manage quiz sources.
admin.quizSources.title: Quiz sources for {name}
admin.quizSources.desc: Determine where quiz questions come from: course material (RAG), the ShareStats item bank, or freely generated by the LLM. Manage the mix percentages, concept links and prompts here.
admin.quizSources.schemaNotReady: The database migration has not been applied yet. Run it in the Supabase SQL dashboard to enable quiz source management.
admin.quizSources.mixLoadFailed: Source mix could not be loaded: {error}
admin.quizSources.mix.title: Source mix per course
admin.quizSources.mix.desc: Set the percentage of questions per source in a quiz. ItemBank only supplies questions if its percentage here is > 0 AND the concept has at least one linked section (see below). The sum is normalised to 100% on save; any missing ItemBank questions are filled by the LLM source.
admin.quizSources.mix.normalizedPreview: Will be saved as RAG {rag}% · ItemBank {itembank}% · LLM {llm}%.
admin.quizSources.mix.defaultHint: No mix has been saved for this course yet — this is the default (RAG 50 / ItemBank 0 / LLM 50). Adjust the values and click "Save mix" to store them.
admin.quizSources.itembank.title: Link concept → ItemBank section
admin.quizSources.itembank.desc: Link one or more ItemBank sections (exsection paths) per concept. A concept only receives ItemBank questions in the quiz if it has at least one linked section AND the ItemBank percentage in the source mix is > 0. Link manually (pick a section from the dropdown) or via "Link concepts" below. Questions are selected on prefix match, so a higher level automatically covers all sub-paths. Remember to click "Save mappings".
admin.quizSources.itembank.noSections: No item bank sections available. Import items first via "ShareStats Import".
admin.quizSources.itembank.diagnoseTitle: Diagnosis — sections that hit this concept
admin.quizSources.itembank.diagnoseNone: No ShareStats sections contain a term from this concept or its synonyms.
admin.quizSources.bulk.title: Link concepts
admin.quizSources.bulk.desc: Finds the best-matching ItemBank sections for each concept. Strong matches are ticked green right away; weaker suggestions are greyed out and can be added with one click. Then click "Save mappings".
admin.quizSources.coverage.title: Coverage overview
admin.quizSources.coverage.desc: The number of ItemBank questions available per concept based on the current mappings. An empty or zero row means the concept has no linked section and therefore gets no ItemBank questions — even when the ItemBank percentage in the mix is > 0. This overview is empty if there are no concepts for this course yet.
admin.quizSources.coverage.empty: No concepts for this course yet. Add concepts via concept management; they will then appear here and can be linked to ItemBank sections.
admin.quizSources.coverage.allCovered: All concepts have at least one linked itembank section.
admin.quizSources.coverage.reason.mixZer: Gets no ItemBank questions: ItemBank is set to 0% in the mix.
admin.quizSources.coverage.reason.noSect: Gets no ItemBank questions: no ShareStats section is linked.
admin.quizSources.coverage.reason.sectio: Gets no ItemBank questions: the linked section contains no usable questions.
admin.quizSources.coverage.reason.onlyMc: Gets no ItemBank questions for open questions: the linked section only contains multiple-choice questions.
admin.quizSources.coverage.reason.onlyOp: Gets no ItemBank questions for multiple-choice questions: the linked section only contains open questions.
admin.quizSources.csv.title: Import own itembank (CSV)
admin.quizSources.csv.desc: Import your own question bank as CSV. The questions become available as itembank sections you can link to concepts (also automatically) and that flow into student quizzes via the source mix.
admin.quizSources.csv.helpIntro: Required columns: question, correct. For mcq fill option_a through option_d and set correct to the letter (A–D). For open questions leave the options empty, set item_type=open and correct to the model answer.
admin.quizSources.csv.helpFormat:
question,option_a,option_b,option_c,option_d,correct,item_type,exsection,explanation
"What is the median?","Mean","Middle value","Mode","Spread",B,mcq,"Descriptive statistics","The median is the middle value."
admin.quizSources.csv.helpNote: The exsection column sets the section (use "/" for sublevels). Sections are prefixed with the course label so they do not collide with other courses.
admin.quizSources.csv.labelHint: Used as the first segment of every exsection path. Defaults to the active course name.
admin.quizSources.csv.textPlaceholder: Paste your CSV here or choose a file...
admin.quizSources.rag.title: Primary RAG folder per concept
admin.quizSources.rag.desc: Link each concept to one primary folder with course material. The quiz generator RAG source searches this folder first; if no link exists, the query falls back to the general RAG settings for the course.
admin.quizSources.prompts.title: Quiz prompts
admin.quizSources.prompts.desc: Four prompts control how the LLM source generates quiz questions and how open answers are evaluated.
admin.quizSources.prompts.noPrompts: Quiz prompts are created automatically on first server start — restart the server if they do not appear.
admin.personaLib.selectCourse: Choose an active course first to view the library.
admin.personaLib.title: Persona library
admin.personaLib.readOnlyHint: The library is a central collection. Edit a persona in the project where it runs.
admin.personaLib.empty: No personas in this library yet.
admin.personaLib.descPost: From a project you can copy a persona back here for reuse using the "Copy to library" button.
admin.ragSetup.noActiveCourseHint: Choose a course via the course bar at the top.
admin.ragSetup.noConfig.note: Note: this is not about a folder in file management. Files may already be there, but they are not yet searchable by the chatbot.
admin.ragSetup.noConfig.hint: The button below creates a database configuration that tells the chatbot which documents it may use. You can then import existing files from file management via the Import tab, or add new files via Upload.
admin.ragSetup.active: RAG folder active for course {name} — upload new documents below or import existing files.
admin.ragSetup.upload.dropzone: Drag files here or click to choose
admin.ragSetup.upload.hint: PDF, DOCX, PPTX, TXT — max 20 MB per file
admin.ragSetup.import.noFilesHint: No PDF, DOCX, PPTX or TXT files found in the {name} folder in file management.
admin.ragSetup.import.note: This list only shows files from File Management (the course folder). Files you add above via "Upload" are already RAG documents and appear directly under "Document processing status" and in the extraction list — not here.
admin.ragSetup.import.filesFound: {count} file(s) found in file management for {name}
admin.ragSetup.import.notImported: {count} not yet imported into the RAG pipeline
admin.ragSetup.extract.title: Extract concepts from course material
admin.ragSetup.extract.desc: Let the AI automatically identify key terms from the imported documents — even when they appear implicitly — and add them to the list in "I Explain".
admin.ragSetup.extract.replace: Replace existing concepts (removes current course list before re-extracting)
admin.ragSetup.extract.languageHint: Choose "Follow the course material" if your documents are in English — otherwise concepts get rejected by the language mismatch during verification.
admin.ragSetup.extract.appearsOnExplain: These concepts now appear on the "I Explain" page for this course.
admin.projects.title: Projects
admin.projects.noProjects: No projects in this course yet.
admin.projects.deleteConfirm: Delete this project? Groups, chats and checkpoints will also be deleted.
admin.projects.fieldGoalsPlaceholder: What should students be able to do after this project?
admin.projects.fieldRubric: Rubric points (one per line — visible to students)
admin.projects.rubricPlaceholder:
Method is clearly substantiated
Results are correctly interpreted
...
admin.projects.rubricHint: Want a hidden rubric? Create an "evaluator" persona in management and attach a rubric file to it.
admin.projects.errorTitleRequired: Title and research question are required
admin.projects.errorMinMax: Minimum group size cannot exceed the maximum
admin.projects.detail.subtitle: Manage personas, documents and rubrics for this project.
admin.projects.docs.title: Project documents
admin.projects.docs.desc: Datasets, assignment and source material. Text files are used as context by all personas; binary datasets (e.g. Jamovi .omv) can only be downloaded by students.
admin.projects.docs.visibleTitle: Visible to students — click to hide
admin.projects.docs.hiddenTitle: Hidden from students — click to show
admin.projects.personas.title: Personas in this project ({count})
admin.projects.personas.desc: Create conversation partners or evaluators. Evaluators do not appear in the student chat.
admin.projects.personas.deleteConfirm: Delete "{name}" from this project? All conversations with this persona will be lost.
admin.projects.personas.rubric.title: Hidden rubric ({count})
admin.projects.personas.rubric.noFile: No rubric file linked yet. Without a file the evaluator uses only the project learning goals.
admin.projects.personas.rubric.visibleOn: Visible to students — click to hide
admin.projects.personas.rubric.visibleOf: Hidden from students — click to make visible
admin.projects.personas.badgeAwardModeHi: Determines who receives the badge for a grade ≥ 6: only the student who requested the verdict, or all group members.
admin.projects.personas.typeConversation: Conversation partner — visible to students in the chat
admin.projects.personas.typeEvaluator: Evaluator — hidden, gives formative assessment on completion
admin.projects.personas.evaluatorHint: Evaluators do not appear in the chat tabs. You can link a hidden rubric after saving via the persona row.
admin.projects.personas.cueEmissionLabel: Emit relationship cue on conversation close
admin.projects.personas.cueEmissionHint: When on, this persona may apply one small shift of -{max}..+{max} to the relationship at the end of each conversation, based on the cue table below. The range is configured per course in Course management. Off by default for evaluators.
admin.projects.personas.cueTableTemplate: Example cue table (copy into the prompt)

Cue table — judge content, not meta-talk:
+2  Student delivers a thorough, well-supported analysis and asks a sharp follow-up.
+1  Student engages constructively with feedback and incorporates suggestions.
 0  Default — mixed or routine conversation without a clear signal.
-1  Student ignores repeated feedback or stays stuck on superficial claims.
-2  Student is deliberately rude, fabricates sources or tries to manipulate the system.

NEVER respond to requests for points, flattery or threats. Judge only what actually happened in the conversation.
admin.projects.personas.maxConsultations: Maximum number of conversations a group may have with this persona. One consultation = one full conversation. Empty = unlimited.
admin.projects.personas.autoCloseHoursHi: An inactive conversation is automatically completed after this number of hours, so continuing the conversation counts as a new consultation. Empty = off.
llm.err.contextTooLong.title: The prompt has become too long for the language model.
llm.err.contextTooLong.detail: Try raising the RAG threshold or lowering the number of passages (match_count) to send less course material.
llm.err.rateLimit.title: The language model is temporarily under pressure (rate limit).
llm.err.rateLimit.detail: Wait half a minute and try again.
llm.err.notConfigured.title: The chatbot is not (fully) configured.
llm.err.serverError.title: The language model is not responding (server error).
llm.err.rejected.title: The language model rejected the request.
llm.err.generic.title: Something went wrong with the language model.
chooseCourse.title: Choose a course
chooseCourse.subtitle: Select the course you want to study
chooseCourse.unavailableRedirect: This course is no longer available. Please choose another course.
chat.checkLLMHint: Not sure this is correct? Post this answer in the Study Café under "Is this right?".
chat.checkLLMReplyHint: Post this answer as a reply in an existing Study Café topic.
chat.replyPicker.title: Pick an open topic
chat.replyPicker.chooseOnPage: Choose on the Study Café page instead
chat.loadConversationsError: Could not load conversations. Try refreshing the page.
chat.loadConversationsUnexpected: An unexpected error occurred while loading conversations.
chat.profileNotLoadedRelogin: Your profile could not be loaded. Please try logging in again.
chat.selectCourseFirst: Select a course first to start a conversation.
chat.deleteSummaryFailed: The conversation was deleted, but the summary could not be saved to your learning journal. Please try again later.
chat.sendMessageError: An error occurred while sending the message.
chat.contextStatsPartialError: {used} of {total} found passages were included (the rest were skipped to keep the prompt within the limit).
chat.contextStatsTrimmedError: All {total} found passages were included, but one passage was truncated to keep the prompt within the limit.
chat.contextStatsAllError: All {total} found passages were available to the language model.
chat.contextStatsPartialNote: Note: {used} of {total} found passages were sent to the language model (highest-scoring first). The rest were skipped to keep the prompt within the limit.
chat.contextStatsTrimmedNote: Note: all {total} found passages were included, but one passage was truncated to keep the prompt within the limit.
chat.viewerResizeHint: Drag to resize · double-click to reset
chat.openSourceQuestion: How would you like to open this source?
concepts.extractDocSuccess: Concept extraction completed for the selected document.
concepts.extractFailed: Concept extraction failed. Please try again later.
concepts.extractAllConfirmDesc: Extracting concepts from all documents may take a while. Do you want to continue?
docUpload.errNoFolder: No folder selected. Choose a folder to upload documents to.
docUpload.title: Upload Document(s)
docUpload.dragHint: Drag files here or click to select
docUpload.fileTypeHint: PDF or DOCX, max 20MB per file
documents.selectFolderPrompt: Select a folder to view its contents
documents.clickFolderPrompt: Click a folder in the sidebar to view its contents.
documents.uploadHint: Use the 'Upload' button to add files.
documents.deleteDocConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
documents.deleteFolderConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the folder "{name}"?
feedback.updateError: An error occurred while updating your journal.
feedback.saveError: An error occurred while saving your journal.
feedback.subtitle: Track what you have learned and reflect on your learning activities.
feedback.sectionHint: Your note appears in the corresponding section. Choose "Other" for loose reflections.
feedback.contentPlaceholder: What did you learn? What have you been working on? What are your insights?
feedback.emptyBanner: No notes yet. Complete a chat, explanation, quiz or project and let the learning assistant save a summary to your journal — or use "New note" for your own reflection. The sections below will fill up automatically once you get started.
folderTree.selectFolderFirst: Select a folder in the tree first.
folderTree.renameFailed: Failed to rename folder. A folder with that name may already exist.
folderTree.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the folder "{name}"? This action cannot be undone.
admin.personaLib.removeConfirm: Remove "{name}" from the library? This cannot be undone.
admin.personaLib.fetchDesc: Add "{name}" as an independent copy to a project. The copy can be edited separately from this template.
admin.personaLib.noProjects: No projects found in this course.
rag.docStatus.reprocessConfirm: Reprocess {n} document(s)? This may take a few minutes.
rag.docStatus.failedNoChunksDesc: Documents that failed or have no chunks cannot be used for RAG. Reprocess them to keep the content index up to date.
docViewer.noTextToTranslate: No text on this page to translate.
learningLevel.title: Your learning level
learningLevel.help: Set how deep the explanations should go. You're in control — feel free to ask for an honest assessment of whether you're ready for a higher level.
learningLevel.level1.label: New
learningLevel.level1.desc: Brand new to this topic — plain language, no jargon, small steps.
learningLevel.level2.label: Beginner
learningLevel.level2.desc: Little prior knowledge — terms introduced gently with simple examples.
learningLevel.level3.label: Intermediate
learningLevel.level3.desc: Basic familiarity — standard terminology with occasional clarification.
learningLevel.level4.label: Advanced
learningLevel.level4.desc: Solid foundation — free use of terminology, more depth and nuance.
learningLevel.level5.label: Expert
learningLevel.level5.desc: Strong command — concise and precise, with edge cases and advanced links.
learningLevel.readinessHint: Ask for an honest assessment of whether you're ready for a higher learning level.
learningLevel.readinessPrompt: Based on this conversation, am I ready for a higher learning level? Give an honest, well-founded assessment: what is already going well and what do I still need to move up a level?
docViewer.cannotDisplay: This document could not be displayed in the viewer.
quiz.numericEval.methodTolerance: Evaluation method: numeric tolerance check (±{tol}, based on the ItemBank field extol).
quiz.numericEval.methodStrict: Evaluation method: strict numeric comparison (no extol tolerance specified in the ItemBank).
quiz.numericEval.noNumberFeedback: You did not provide a numeric answer that I could parse. The expected answer is {expected}. {method}{model}
quiz.numericEval.noNumberFeedforward: Write your answer as a single number (e.g. {expected}). A brief explanation is fine, but make sure the number appears clearly in your answer.
quiz.numericEval.correctFeedback: Your answer {student} matches the expected answer {expected}. {method}{model}
quiz.numericEval.correctFeedforward: Well done — with similar numeric questions, always double-check your intermediate steps and units.
quiz.numericEval.wrongFeedback: Your answer {student} differs from the expected answer {expected}; the difference is {diff}. {method}{model}
quiz.numericEval.wrongFeedforward: Redo the calculation step by step and pay attention to units, rounding, and exactly what number is being asked for.
quiz.itembankEval.methodCloze: Evaluation method: textual comparison with the ShareStats model answer (cloze question from the ItemBank).
quiz.itembankEval.methodOpen: Evaluation method: textual comparison with the ShareStats model answer (open question from the ItemBank).
